questions.question: Which of the following is true concerning the production of human insulin in E. coli?

options.A: The A and B chains are produced separately in E. coli and then extracted and linked in vitro.
options.B: The complete proinsulin is synthesized and cleaved inside E. coli.
options.C: E. coli directly secretes fully formed, active insulin.
options.D: E. coli cannot produce human insulin proteins.

questions.question: The term 'in vitro' refers to processes that occur:

options.A: Outside a living organism, typically in an artificial environment like a test tube.
options.B: Inside the living cells of a whole organism.
options.D: In a vacuum only.

questions.question: Why is Taq polymerase essential for the Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR)?

options.A: It is thermostable and does not denature during the high-temperature denaturation phase.
options.B: It works much faster than any human polymerase.
options.D: It does not require template DNA.

questions.question: Which vector is most suitable for cloning the largest fragments of human DNA, such as during the Human Genome Project?

options.A: YACs (Yeast Artificial Chromosomes) and BACs (Bacterial Artificial Chromosomes)
options.B: pBR322
options.D: Ti Plasmid

questions.question: An organism that contains and expresses a gene from another species is referred to as:

options.A: Transgenic
options.B: Mutant
options.D: Chimaera

questions.question: Which process describes the natural ability of agrobacterium to transfer T-DNA into the plant genome?

options.A: Genetic colonization / Transformation
options.B: Transduction
options.D: Binary fission

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'insertional inactivation' in genetic engineering?

options.A: To screen and separate recombinant colonies from non-recombinant colonies
options.B: To destroy the host cell's DNA
options.C: To activate the toxic genes of a pathogen
options.D: To stop the replication of the plasmid completely

questions.question: Beta-galactosidase assay (blue-white screening) is used to identify recombinant cells. In this method, non-recombinant colonies appear:

options.A: Blue, because the lacZ gene is intact and produces beta-galactosidase.
options.B: White, because the lacZ gene is disrupted by the insert.
options.C: Red, because they metabolize the indicator dye.
options.D: Colorless.

questions.question: Before a genetically modified organism can be released into the environment in India, it must clear the scrutiny of:

options.A: GEAC
options.B: NDDB
options.D: The Ministry of Health

questions.question: Which ethical argument is most frequently raised against the patenting of indigenous plant varieties by foreign multinational corporations?

options.A: It ignores the traditional knowledge and prior breeding efforts of local farmers (Biopiracy).
options.B: It leads to the extinction of the plant.
options.C: The patents are always scientifically invalid.
options.D: It violates the laws of thermodynamics.

questions.question: In forensic science, the precision of DNA fingerprinting is primarily derived from identifying variations in:

options.A: The number of repetitive DNA sequences (minisatellites) specific to an individual.
options.B: The exact sequences of genes encoding standard proteins like insulin.
options.C: The number of chromosomes present in the cell.
options.D: The sequence of mitochondrial DNA only.

questions.question: Enzyme Replacement Therapy (ERT) for ADA deficiency involves:

options.A: Periodic intravenous injection of functional ADA enzyme into the patient.
options.B: A one-time bone marrow transplant.
options.C: Surgical removal of the thymus gland.
options.D: The use of heavy antibiotics.

questions.question: A 'shuttle vector' is a specialized plasmid that:

options.A: Can replicate in two different host organisms (e.g., E. coli and yeast).
options.B: Shuttles proteins across the cell membrane.
options.D: Contains only antibiotic resistance genes and no replication origin.

questions.question: Sparging in a bioreactor refers to:

options.A: Passing sterile air bubbles through the culture medium to ensure oxygenation.
options.B: Rapidly cooling the reactor.
options.D: Filtering the final product.

questions.question: Continuous culture systems in bioreactors are used primarily to:

options.A: Maintain the cells in their physiological most active log/exponential phase.
options.B: Force the cells into a dormant stationary phase.
options.C: Extract the product without adding fresh media.
options.D: Kill the cells at the end of the batch.

questions.question: Humulin, as a milestone in biotechnology, proved that:

options.A: Bacterial systems could be successfully engineered to produce functional mammalian hormones.
options.B: Insulin derived from pigs was fundamentally toxic.
options.D: Humans could synthesize their own insulin without a pancreas.

questions.question: Transgenic pigs have been heavily researched for the purpose of 'Xenotransplantation', which means:

options.A: Transplanting organs from animals to humans.
options.B: Creating new pig breeds for farming.
options.C: Extracting pig insulin for human use.
options.D: Transferring human organs into pigs.

questions.question: Recombinant DNA technology fundamentally relies on the universality of:

options.A: The genetic code.
options.B: The immune system.
options.D: Protein folding mechanisms across all kingdoms.

questions.question: During recombinant DNA experiments, DNA is precipitated out of solution for purification by adding:

options.A: Chilled ethanol.
options.B: Hot water.
options.D: Chloroform.

questions.question: Restriction enzymes produce 'sticky ends' on DNA to facilitate:

options.A: The efficient formation of hydrogen bonds between complementary overhangs prior to ligation.
options.B: The rapid degradation of the DNA by exonucleases.
options.C: The permanent separation of the DNA strands.
options.D: The immediate expression of the gene without a promoter.
